title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A benefit eligibility notification program. They need leak-resistant consumers, same-generation delivery decisions, bounded prefetch, transactional inbox effects, typed failure classification, delayed retry budgets, parking, and an audited replay tool. Planned artifact: Deliver Duplicate-Safe Benefit Effect Processor with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Bound concurrent handlers by CPU, memory, message bytes, downstream pools, deadlines, tenant fairness, and shutdown capacity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Cancel Inflight Boundary
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go messaging team handles case 3047 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound dead-letter history, stop cycles, and separate retry tiers from authorized parking; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then close the owning channel before one delivery decision. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Cancel new deliveries, then await, finish, nack, or abandon in-flight work under a deadline before closing the channel; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

What must be established before implementation?
